title: subagent: run_subagent ignores memory_db, breaking memory_* tools for spawned agents
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/subagent.rs:936 (run_subagent tool dispatch)
## Violation
```rust
let _session_guard = crate::tools::SessionIdGuard::set(&agent_id);
let result = crate::tools::execute_tool_with_memory(&tc, None, Some(&permission_mgr));
```

The subagent unconditionally passes `None` for `memory_db`. Every memory_* tool invoked by the agent will receive `None` and either no-op or error. The session id is bound via `SessionIdGuard::set(&agent_id)` on the line above — clearly intent was to scope memory operations per agent — but the actual `memory_db` handle is dropped.

## Mandated refactor
Thread `&MemoryDb` (or `Option<&MemoryDb>` if the agent type opts out) through `SubagentConfig` into `run_subagent`. At the call site, pass `memory_db` instead of `None`. If the policy is "no memory access for subagents", document that explicitly and remove the SessionIdGuard above, which is dead code under that policy.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
